title: The after effects of 2nd Impact Flooding
display_title: The after effects of 2nd Impact Flooding
wikitext:
[]: The after effects of 2nd Impact (Flooding)             Quote Post by xPearse » Thu Feb 19, 2015 9:42 am I'm very curious about this, just how much of the earth was flooded because of 2nd impact? I mean, when it shows you the world from space in the show it looks very much unchanged by the water levels that rose 15 years prior. Is that just a goof or am I missing something? Heck, most of Europe would be underwater due to it being low land, so how is the UK still one of the top dogs in the world? Wouldn't they have suffered heavy damage because of 2nd impact, and I know everyone has recovered from 2nd Impact at the time the show takes place, but by then the balance of power would have shifted greatly. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Feb 19, 2015 10:00 am Here's an easy way to visualise the effects of any selected rise up to 60m in the level of the oceans: [url]http://flood.firetree.net/[/url] You will note that most of Europe (sorry Belgium, Netherlands and Denmark!) remains dry at 60m, and even much of the UK.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 19, 2015 10:56 am pwhodges wrote: You will note that most of Europe (sorry Belgium, Netherlands and Denmark!) remains dry at 60m, and even much of the UK.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Feb 19, 2015 11:58 am But at least the Aral Sea will return!

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Thu Feb 19, 2015 8:12 pm Here's an easy way to visualise the effects of any selected rise up to 60m in the level of the oceans: http://flood.firetree.net/

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 20, 2015 1:36 am Related thread -- http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13329/Post-2I-Sea-Level-Rise-and-Climate-Change/

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Feb 20, 2015 7:55 am pwhodges wrote: Here's an easy way to visualise the effects of any selected rise up to 60m in the level of the oceans: [url]http://flood.firetree.net/[/url] You will note that most of Europe (sorry Belgium, Netherlands and Denmark!) remains dry at 60m, and even much of the UK.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sat Feb 21, 2015 5:52 pm Alright, here's a worse problem: Asuka leaves from Wilhelmshaven, which is... pretty much underwater at only one meter's rise.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Feb 21, 2015 5:56 pm Good catch!

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Feb 21, 2015 9:11 pm Lavinius wrote: Alright, here's a worse problem: Asuka leaves from Wilhelmshaven, which is... pretty much underwater at only one meter's rise.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Feb 21, 2015 9:22 pm Wilhelmshaven simply operates like Venice, Italy after Second Impact. Problem solved. (Venice, on the other hand, finally sinks in the drink.)

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Feb 21, 2015 10:59 pm As far as the US is concerned, the state that seems to have the most risk of getting totally submerged is Delaware, though Florida and Louisiana don't look to fare too well either.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sun Feb 22, 2015 1:06 pm Guy Nacks wrote: As far as the US is concerned, the state that seems to have the most risk of getting totally submerged is Delaware, though Florida and Louisiana don't look to fare too well either.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Feb 22, 2015 2:10 pm Compiling_Autumn wrote: More specifically, those of us in Virginia lose Norfolk, Quantico and the capital. Basically our biggest Navy base, Marine base, and of course the Pentagon.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 22, 2015 2:52 pm I vaguely recall that the submerged city seen in episode 08 should not be submerged, but someone else will have to double-check this.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Feb 22, 2015 11:22 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Wilhelmshaven simply operates like Venice, Italy after Second Impact. Problem solved. (Venice, on the other hand, finally sinks in the drink.)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 23, 2015 7:01 am Yokohama is far too deeply submerged - as in a couple of hundred meters under water. There's no way that should've been possible even after the mess post-2I. I vaguely recall that the N2 weapons used during the 2000 war caused sections of the coast to subside, or something like that...not that it made any sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 25, 2015 10:33 am pwhodges wrote: Good catch!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Feb 25, 2015 11:07 am I can comprehend sea level rise due to ice melting, but my brain gets stuck on sea level declining due to the destruction of a continent. Are we talking destruction as in "blown into itty bitty chunks", in which case all of the matter comprising said continent is still on Earth and hypothetically displacing sea water, or destruction as in vaporized into gas or atomized into energy or something? The answer will probably make me feel stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Feb 25, 2015 11:13 am If 2I blew enough of the ice into space, or vaporized enough ocean water to create weird stuff like the pillars of salt, it might actually have brought the sea level down. But Antarctica is actually a continent that's depressed under the sheer weight of the ice covering it and if the ice covering it were to be rid of, it would rise over tens or hundreds of thousands of years. So...I'd say it's pretty messed up.

Article: The after effects of 2nd Impact Flooding
11:13 am If 2I blew enough of the ice into space, or vaporized enough ocean water to create weird stuff like the pillars of salt, it might actually have brought the sea level down. But Antarctica is actually a continent that's depressed under the sheer weight of the ice covering it and if the ice covering it were to be rid of, it would rise over tens or hundreds of thousands of years. So...I'd say it's pretty messed up.
